title: Coverage, Thresholds, and Meaningful Evidence · Gate Lens: Coverage Review
summary: Set coverage policy that exposes risk without encouraging empty tests. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gates coverage review increment in a changed-code coverage and exception policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Pair coverage gaps with mutation, defect, and risk evidence.
Prevent new untested behavior while ratcheting legacy coverage.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# Risk-profiled repository gate portfolio
# Each gate needs purpose, command, failure fixture, owner, and suppression policy.

steps.title: Predict Coverage Review
steps.instruction:
Use a changed theory case derived from this module: gates coverage. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Use a changed theory case derived from this module: gates coverage. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. Before reading, predict which action preserves the Coverage Review contract.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for pair coverage gaps with mutation, defect, and risk evidence.

content.text: Pair coverage gaps with mutation, defect, and risk evidence. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Compare plausible approaches against the decision, constraints, evidence quality, and cost of being wrong.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: gates coverage. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Coverage Review, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a changed-code coverage and exception policy.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: The highest-coverage test suite is automatically best. This fails the Coverage Review contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Build Coverage Review evidence
steps.instruction: Append a gates-coverage-review-1f3v3r: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Coverage Review into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
gates-coverage-review-1f3v3r:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

stimulus.title: Coverage Review failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: The highest-coverage test suite is automatically best.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Pair coverage gaps with mutation, defect, and risk evidence.

options.text: Reconstruct the conditions for Coverage Review, gather a counterexample, repair the artifact, and add a regression case.
Keep the artifact unchanged and rewrite the explanation so the current output appears intentional.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Defend the Coverage Review model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Coverage Review applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Pair coverage gaps with mutation, defect, and risk evidence.
